target.name: QuantumScape
target.founder: Jagdeep Singh, Tim Holme, Fritz Prinz

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
QuantumScape is a public-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector currently valued at $5B+, having raised $2B+ to date. The comparable universe consists of 10 peers in the same sector and valuation tier, comprising 1 public company (LanzaTech) and 8 private companies including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

The peer-median method is applicable and represents the primary triangulation approach. The 10-company peer set yields a median valuation of $3.4B, with a range spanning $1.0B at the low end to $6.0B at the high end. This median reflects valuations across companies at varying funding stages, from Commonwealth Fusion Systems at Series B ($5.0B) to Form Energy at Series G ($3.5B). The dispersion in the peer set is substantial, indicating significant valuation variance within the Climate & Energy frontier-tech cohort.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $2.9B to $3.9B, with a median of $3.4B. QuantumScape's current $5B+ valuation sits above the peer median by approximately 47%, reflecting either a premium attributed to its public status and liquidity or divergent investor views on its technology trajectory and market timing relative to the broader peer set. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of valuations in early-stage energy technology, limited public comparable data, and variability in funding stage progression across peers.
